PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Nov-30-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances in-person:
Gary E. Dutton (Self-Represented Petitioner);
Michael Sandgren, Esq. (Gary Dutton's son-in-law/Interested Person).

The court addressed the petition for probate and Mr. Dutton proceeding in pro per. Mr. Dutton confirmed for the Court that he understands his duties and liabilities, that a separate checking account needs to be maintained during the pendency of the probate, that all the probate assets are to be kept separate and protected, that the creditor claims must be paid, and that distributions cannot be made without a court order. Mr. Dutton noted that the matter seems pretty straightforward, that he doesn't see anything complicated re: this probate, and inquired about the letters. The clerk informed Mr. Dutton that the letters will be issued by the clerk's office in Room 103.

At the conclusion of the comments, the Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed with limited authority (order signed in open court). The will dated 5/27/1980 is admitted to probate by this order. Bond is fixed at $20,000.00. Isidoor Bornstein is appointed the probate referee.

A hearing on the status of the filing of a final inventory and appraisal is set for April 18, 2023, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

A hearing on the status of closing of the estate is set for December 19, 2023, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ceclia Castellanos. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Lozado. Matter not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Oct-25-2022 Continued To Nov-30-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
